summary: 7 items surfaced from today's HN comment threads.

items.name: Honker
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Honker – Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN Semantics for SQLite
items.summary: SQLite extension that adds Postgres-style NOTIFY/LISTEN and durable queues.
items.why_included: Show HN with strong practical appeal and concrete discussion of low-latency, atomic messaging.
items.evidence: HN comments praised the "one db and no daemon" model, low-latency reactivity, and atomic rollback with durable work queues.

items.name: Kamal
items.summary: Deployment tool for shipping web apps to VMs and bare metal.
items.why_included: A commenter gave a concrete Kubernetes alternative they actually use in practice.
items.evidence: One commenter said they nuked Kubernetes, moved to a single Debian VM, and used Kamal with Docker.

items.name: SourceHut
items.summary: Email-first open-source forge for git repos, CI, and collaboration.
items.why_included: A reliability thread surfaced it as a practical GitHub alternative people are considering now.
items.evidence: A commenter said they would move to SourceHut because it supports contributions by email without an account requirement.

items.name: ImHex
items.source_story_title: Your hex editor should color-code bytes
items.summary: Open-source hex editor with structured overlays and parser support.
items.why_included: The hex-editor thread repeatedly recommended it for real reverse-engineering workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters praised ImHex for structured overlays, built-in parsers, a pattern language, and active maintenance.

items.name: NixOS
items.summary: Declarative Linux platform for reproducible builds and homelabs.
items.why_included: A reliability thread highlighted it as a strong baseline for self-hosted infrastructure.
items.evidence: A commenter said "NixOS really is that good, particularly for homelabs" amid the GitHub outage discussion.

items.name: Pebble
items.source_story_title: A DIY Watch You Can Actually Wear
items.summary: Comeback smartwatch with e-paper screens and unusually long battery life.
items.why_included: The watch thread singled it out as nearly ideal for people who want a simple smartwatch.
items.evidence: A commenter called the new Pebble nearly perfect and said the main miss was no O2 sensor.

items.name: GPT-5.5
items.summary: OpenAI's latest model release for coding, research, and long tasks.
items.why_included: Major AI launch with comment-level praise beyond simple benchmark chatter.
items.evidence: Commenters said it feels strong at long-horizon tasks and discussed Codex workflow improvements and pricing.
